bio: Product Manager at Microsoft.

MSc. Facundo Santiago is a Principal Product Manager in MicrosoftAI. He has more than 10 years designing, implementing, and developing intelligent systems based on machine learning techniques. He currently works in the Agents Orchestration and Frameworks group, responsible of evolving the multi-agent capabilities of Microsoft Foundry and how open-source frameworks can be used to develop state-of-the-art agentic applications across solutions. Facundo's academic background includes serving as an Adjunct Master's Professor at the Universidad de Buenos Aires (Argentina).

Rong Lu is a Principal Product Manager at Microsoft, building developer experiences in VS Code and Azure for data science and AI. She began her career at Microsoft as a software engineer after earning her master’s degree in computer science, later transitioning into product management. Over the past 18 years, she has led the development of Visual Studio and VS Code experiences for game developers, data scientists, and most recently, AI engineers. Rong has been a frequent conference speaker since 2007.
Craig Loewen works on bringing great Linux workflows and experiences to Windows with the Windows Subsystem for Linux and other developer and AI focused tooling!.

Robert is a PM in the Windows Cloud engineering org, focused on provisioning and operationalizing Windows environments through AVD and Windows 365. With roots in ALM and modern DevOps disciplines, he works at the intersection of process, platform, and execution—exploring how agent‑driven approaches like Computer‑Using Agents can reshape how enterprise environments are built, deployed, and managed.
Abe Omorogbe is a Senior Product Manager on Microsoft's Azure PostgreSQL AI team, where he drives the development of AI/ML, Vector Search, and GenAI capabilities for Azure Database for PostgreSQL.
Aaron is a Developer Advocate at Microsoft with a passion for .NET, GitHub Copilot, whacky ideas and running long distances.
Ellie is a staff product manager at GitHub where she works on Copilot cloud agent.

Yun Jung Choi is a Senior Product Manager at Microsoft focused on GitHub Copilot for Azure and Azure MCP, simplifying how developers build, deploy, and manage cloud solutions through AI‑driven workflows in Azure.

Developer on the VS Code team whose current focus is on agentic scenarios with Claude via GitHub Copilot, and maintaining a strong MCP Authentication story. Outside of work, I do improv & am in to specialty coffee.
Mads is the lead designer of the C# Programming Language and a Principal Architect at Microsoft.
Product leader for Azure DevOps, focused on Azure Boards, Repos, GitHub integration, and bringing AI-driven experiences to the platform.
Mads Kristensen is a Principal Product Manager on the Visual Studio team at Microsoft, where he focuses on improving the everyday experience of developers using the IDE.

Pamela Fox is a human that loves to learn, teach, and create. She's currently a Cloud Advocate in Python at Microsoft, where she helps developers use Python with Azure, Microsoft Foundry, VS Code, and GitHub.
Charles is the Director of Product Management for Postgres and MySQL on Azure, which includes the managed services portfolio as well as contributions to related open source projects.
Josh is an engineer on the VS Code team building AI developer tools.
Product Lead for Windows ML.

Dave is a Sr. DevOps Advocate for GitHub & Azure DevOps. He has led executive briefings with leaders from 100’s of GitHub’s enterprise customers. He enables software development teams to become as innovative, efficient and productive as possible.
Product Manager working on GitHub Copilot and VS Code.

Ayca Bas is a Senior Developer Advocate at Microsoft, helping developers build intelligent solutions with AI. She works on flagship programs and creates hands-on content that makes advanced AI concepts practical and accessible.

Felipe brings 7+ years of experience in cloud marketplaces, driving digital transformation and cloud strategy optimization. Felipe leads category strategy, guiding partners and customers to unlock marketplace value for efficiency and growth.
Principal Developer Advocate at Microsoft. Founder of the Global AI Community and AgentCon. Building with Microsoft Foundry and connecting developers across the globe.

Satya Nadella is Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of Microsoft. Before being named CEO in February 2014, Nadella held leadership roles in both enterprise and consumer businesses across the company.
 
Joining Microsoft in 1992, he quickly became known as a leader who could span a breadth of technologies and businesses to transform some of Microsoft’s biggest product offerings.
 
Most recently, Nadella was executive vice president of Microsoft’s Cloud and Enterprise group. In this role he led the transformation to the cloud infrastructure and services business, which outperformed the market and took share from competition. Previously, Nadella led R&D for the Online Services Division and was vice president of the Microsoft Business Division. Before joining Microsoft, Nadella was a member of the technology staff at Sun Microsystems.
 
Originally from Hyderabad, India, Nadella lives in Bellevue, Washington, with his family. He earned a bachelor’s degree in electrical engineering from Mangalore University, a master’s degree in computer science from the University of Wisconsin – Milwaukee and a master’s degree in business administration from the University of Chicago. Nadella serves on the board of trustees to his alma mater the University of Chicago.

jobTitle: VP Products and AI Futurist, Core AI
bio: Marco Casalaina is VP Products of Core AI and AI Futurist at Microsoft. Prior to coming to Microsoft, Marco led the Einstein AI team at Salesforce. He holds a Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science from Cornell University.

Denzil Ribeiro is a Group Product Manager in the Azure Data PostgreSQL team at Microsoft working on Azure HorizonDB.  Most of his career has been spent at Microsoft working on databases — building products, architecting scalable solutions, and solving complex customer problems involving data.
Cassidy is Senior Director of Developer Advocacy at GitHub. She loves building open source and helping make things better for developers.

Someleze is a young, passionate individual who is part of the Data Advocacy team, which focuses on empowering data communities with the latest tools and technologies for data analysis, visualization, and machine learning.
Maxim is leading Generative AI Developer Experience PM team in Azure Postgres service. He has extensive experience in building Data and AI cloud services and has been with Microsoft for 15 years.

Dustin Campbell is a principal software architect in .NET tooling. Passionate about coding productivity, Dustin's primary focus is on making .NET tooling great and helping design the next version of C# as part of the language design team.

Chris Harris is a Product Manager in Developer Experiences on Microsoft's Core AI team, focused on the intersection of AI coding agents and cloud infrastructure. He works on the Azure Skills Plugin and Azure MCP Server, which together give agents like GitHub Copilot the knowledge and tools to reason about Azure workflows — from service selection to deployment to troubleshooting.

I’m a Product Manager on Visual Studio Code and GitHub Copilot, helping developers build with confidence and joy. I love the editor space—it’s where ideas become real. Every day, millions use VS Code to explore, debug, and ship software.
Principal Software Engineering Manager @ VS Code.
Salil Subbakrishna is a Product Manager at GitHub for Actions. His areas of focus are Developer Experience and CI/CD workflows.

Nikola is a developer on the Windows Developer Platform at Microsoft. His focus is to support developer productivity in building Windows applications across any tech stack and framework, focusing on developer tooling.
